name: time-update

modules.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers
documentation: The following import is to make sure jupyter_ydoc is imported before
jupyterlab_chat, otherwise it leads to circular import because of the
YChat relying on YBaseDoc, and jupyter_ydoc registering YChat from the entry point.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.ask.AskChatHandler
documentation:
Processes messages prefixed with /ask. This actor will
send the message as input to a RetrieverQA chain, that
follows the Retrieval and Generation (RAG) technique to
query the documents from the index, and sends this context
to the LLM to generate the final reply.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._read_config
documentation:
Returns the user's current configuration as a GlobalConfig object.
This should never be sent to the client as it includes API keys. Prefer
self.get_config() for sending the config to the client.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._validate_config
documentation:
Method used to validate the configuration. This is called after every
read and before every write to the config file. Guarantees that the
config file conforms to the JSON Schema, and that the language and
embedding models have authn credentials if specified.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._validate_model
documentation:
Validates a model against the set of allow/blocklists specified by the
traitlets configuration, returning `True` if the model is allowed, and
raising a `BlockedModelError` otherwise. If `raise_exc=False`, this
function returns `False` if the model is not allowed.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._write_config
documentation:
Updates configuration and persists it to disk. This accepts a
complete `GlobalConfig` object, and should not be called publicly.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager.get_config
documentation: this cannot be a property, as the parent Configurable already defines the
self.config attr.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager.persona
documentation:
The current agent persona, set by the selected LM provider. If the
selected LM provider is `None`, this property returns
`JupyternautPersona` by default.

Provides model and embedding provider id along
with the credentials to authenticate providers.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.learn.LearnChatHandler._load
documentation: Loads the vector store.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.learn.LearnChatHandler.delete_and_relearn
documentation:
Delete the vector store and relearn all indexed directories if
necessary. If the embedding model is unchanged, this method does
nothing.

documentation: type:ignore[assignment]

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.context_providers.file.FileContextProvider.get_file_type
documentation:
Determine the file type of the given file path.

Args:
    filepath (str): The file path to analyze.

Returns:
    str: The file type as a string, e.g. '.txt', '.png', '.pdf', etc.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.HandlerRoutingType

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.HandlerRoutingType.routing_method
documentation: The routing method that sends commands to this handler.
Chat handler type, with specific attributes for each

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.SlashCommandRoutingType.slash_id
documentation:
Slash ID for routing a chat command to this handler. Only one handler
may declare a particular slash ID. Must contain only alphanumerics and
underscores.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.on_message
documentation:
Method which receives a human message, calls `self.get_llm_chain()`, and
processes the message via `self.process_message()`, calling
`self.handle_exc()` when an exception is raised.

This is the method called directly in response to new chat messages.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.process_message
documentation:
Processes a human message routed to this chat handler. Chat handlers
(subclasses) must implement this method. Don't forget to call
`self.reply(<response>, chat, message)` at the end!

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block;
any exceptions raised here are caught by `self.handle_exc()`.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.handle_exc
documentation:
Handles an exception raised by `self.process_message()`. A default
implementation is provided, however chat handlers (subclasses) should
implement this method to provide a more helpful error response.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._default_handle_exc
documentation:
The default definition of `handle_exc()`. This is the default used when
the `handle_exc()` excepts.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.reply
documentation:
Adds a message to the YChat shared document that this chat handler is
assigned to. Returns the new message ID.

TODO: Either properly store & use reply state in YChat, or remove the
`human_message` argument here.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.send_help_message
documentation: Sends a help message to all connected clients.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.start_reply_stream
documentation:
Context manager which initializes a `ReplyStream`, opens it, and then
yields the `ReplyStream`. Under this context, developers should call
`reply_stream.write()` on the yielded reply stream to send new string
chunks to the chat.

Once the context is closed, the `ReplyStream` is closed automatically.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.stream_reply
documentation:
Streams a reply to a human message by invoking
`self.llm_chain.astream()`. A LangChain `Runnable` instance must be
bound to `self.llm_chain` before invoking this method.

Arguments
---------
- `input`: The input to your runnable. The type of `input` depends on
the runnable in `self.llm_chain`, but is usually a dictionary whose keys
refer to input variables in your prompt template.

- `_human_message`: The human message being replied to. Currently
unused. TODO: Either re-implement this for v3 or remove it.

 - `_pending_msg` (optional): Changes the default pending message from
"Generating response". Not supported at this time. TODO: Re-implement
this for v3.

- `config` (optional): A `RunnableConfig` object that specifies
additional configuration when streaming from the runnable.

 - `pending_msg` (optional): Changes the default pending message from
"Generating response".

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.id
documentation: ID for this chat handler; should be unique

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.name
documentation: User-facing name of this handler

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.help
documentation:
What this chat handler does, which third-party models it contacts,
the data it returns to the user, and so on, for display in the UI.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.uses_llm
documentation:
Class attribute specifying whether this chat handler uses the LLM
specified by the config. Subclasses should define this. Should be set to
`False` for handlers like `/help`.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.supports_help
documentation:
Class attribute specifying whether this chat handler should
parse the arguments and display help when user queries with
`-h` or `--help`

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._requests_count
documentation:
Class attribute set to the number of requests that Jupyternaut is
currently handling.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.help_message_template
documentation:
Format string template that is used to build the help message. Specified
from traitlets configuration.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.chat_handlers
documentation:
Dictionary of chat handlers. Allows one chat handler to reference other
chat handlers, which is necessary for some use-cases like printing the help
message.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.context_providers
documentation:
Dictionary of context providers. Allows chat handlers to reference
context providers, which can be used to provide context to the LLM.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.message_interrupted
documentation:
Dictionary mapping an agent message identifier to an asyncio Event
which indicates if the message generation/streaming was interrupted.

Base ChatHandler class containing shared methods and attributes used by
multiple chat handler classes.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.GenerationInterrupted
documentation: Exception raised when streaming is cancelled by the user

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.default.DefaultInlineCompletionHandler.handle_request
documentation: Handles an inline completion request without streaming.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_request
documentation:
Handles an inline completion request, without streaming. Subclasses
must define this method and write a reply via `self.reply()`.

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block.
Interface for subclasses

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_stream_request
documentation:
Handles an inline completion request, **with streaming**.
Implementations may optionally define this method. Implementations that
do so should stream replies via successive calls to `self.reply()`.

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.reply
documentation: Write a reply object to the WebSocket connection.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.pre_get
documentation: Handles authentication/authorization.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.get
documentation: Get an event socket.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.on_message
documentation:
Public Tornado method that is called when the client sends a message
over this connection. This should **not** be overriden by subclasses.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_exc
documentation:
Handles an exception raised in either `handle_request()` or
`handle_stream_request()`. This base class provides a default
implementation, which may be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler._handle_request
documentation: Private wrapper around `self.handle_request()`.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler._handle_stream_request
documentation: Private wrapper around `self.handle_stream_request()`.

A Tornado WebSocket handler that receives inline completion requests and
fulfills them accordingly. This class is instantiated once per WebSocket
connection.

functions.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._is_command_call
documentation:
Check if the match is a command call rather than a part of a code block.
This is done by checking if there is an even number of backticks before and
after the match. If there is an odd number of backticks, the match is likely
inside a code block.

documentation: Convert an object to a JSON serializable format

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.callback_handlers.metadata.MetadataCallbackHandler
documentation:
When passed as a callback handler, this stores the LLMResult's
`generation_info` dictionary in the `self.jai_metadata` instance attribute
after the provider fully processes an input.

If used in a streaming chat handler: the `metadata` field of the final
`AgentStreamChunkMessage` should be set to `self.jai_metadata`.

If used in a non-streaming chat handler: the `metadata` field of the
returned `AgentChatMessage` should be set to `self.jai_metadata`.

functions.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.document_loaders.directory.arxiv_to_text
documentation:
Downloads and extracts single tar file from arXiv.
Combines the TeX components into a single file.

Parameters
----------
id : str
    id for the paper, numbers after "arXiv" in arXiv:xxxx.xxxxx

output_dir : str
    directory to save the output file

Returns
-------
output: str
    output path to the downloaded TeX file

Model used to represent the config by ConfigManager. This is exclusive to

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.id
documentation:
ID of the autocomplete option.
Includes the command prefix. E.g. "/clear", "@file".

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.label
documentation:
Text that will be inserted into the prompt when the option is selected.
Includes a space at the end if the option is complete.
Partial suggestions do not include the space and may trigger future suggestions.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.description
documentation: Text next to the option in the autocomplete list.

class_variables.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.only_start
documentation: Whether to command can only be inserted at the start of the prompt.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.history.YChatHistory

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.history.YChatHistory.messages
documentation:
Returns the last `2 * k` messages preceding the latest message. If
`k` is set to `None`, return all preceding messages.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.history.YChatHistory._convert_to_langchain_messages
documentation:
Accepts a list of Jupyter Chat messages, and returns them as a list of
LangChain messages.

An implementation of `BaseChatMessageHistory` that returns the preceding `k`
exchanges (`k * 2` messages) from the given YChat model.

If `k` is set to `None`, then this class returns all preceding messages.

TODO: Consider just defining `k` as the number of messages and default to 4.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension.get_chat
documentation:
Retrieves the YChat instance associated with a room ID. This method
is cached, i.e. successive calls with the same room ID quickly return a
cached value.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension.route_human_message
documentation:
Method that routes an incoming human message to the appropriate chat
handler.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension.stop_extension
documentation:
Public method called by Jupyter Server when the server is stopping.
This calls the cleanup code defined in `self._stop_exception()` inside
an exception handler, as the server halts if this method raises an
exception.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension._stop_extension
documentation:
Private method that defines the cleanup code to run when the server is
stopping.

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension._init_chat_handlers
documentation:
Initializes a set of chat handlers. May accept a YChat instance for
collaborative chats.

TODO: Make `ychat` required once Jupyter Chat migration is complete.

functions.name: timerun.time_change.read_tar_file
documentation:
读取 .tar 文件并列出其中的文件内容。

```
param tar_path

.tar 文件的路径
```

functions.name: timerun.time_change.timestamp_to_datetime
documentation:
将时间戳转换为 datetime 对象。

Args:
    timestamp: 时间戳（秒）。
    tz: 时区字符串，例如 'Asia/Shanghai'、'UTC'。如果为 None，则使用本地时区。

Returns:
    datetime 对象，如果转换失败则返回 None。

functions.name: timerun.time_change.datetime_to_timestamp
documentation:
将 datetime 对象转换为时间戳。

Args:
    dt: datetime 对象。

Returns:
    时间戳（秒），如果转换失败则返回 None。

functions.name: timerun.time_change.str_to_datetime
documentation:
将字符串转换为 datetime 对象。

Args:
    date_str: 日期字符串。
    format_str: 格式字符串，例如 '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'。
    tz: 时区字符串。

Returns:
    datetime 对象，如果转换失败则返回 None。

functions.name: timerun.time_change.datetime_to_str
documentation:
将 datetime 对象转换为字符串。

Args:
    dt: datetime 对象。
    format_str: 格式字符串。

Returns:
    日期字符串，如果转换失败则返回 None。

functions.name: timerun.time_change.timestamp_to_str
documentation: 将时间戳转换为字符串

functions.name: timerun.time_change.str_to_timestamp
documentation: 将字符串转换为时间戳

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.handlers.ProviderHandler

methods.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.handlers.ProviderHandler._filter_blocked_models
documentation:
Satisfy the model-level allow/blocklist by filtering models accordingly.
The provider-level allow/blocklist is already handled in
`AiExtension.initialize_settings()`.

Helper base class used for HTTP handlers hosting endpoints relating to
providers. Wrapper around BaseAPIHandler.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.handlers.GlobalConfigHandler
documentation:
API handler for fetching and setting the
model and emebddings config.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.handlers.SlashCommandsInfoHandler
documentation: List slash commands that are currently available to the user.

classes.name: timerun.jupyter-ai.jupyter_ai.handlers.AutocompleteOptionsHandler
documentation: List context that are currently available to the user.
